column1: The dynamic side of the backend: what gets written as users train. This workbook is the static rules layer, these collections are where user state lives. Reference for the Firebase build, field names are the contract between the app, the generator and the AI layer.

column3: uid, display_name, date_of_birth, age_verified, sex, height_cm, weight_kg, goal_weight_kg, experience, goal, focal_points[] (max 2), days_available[], session_length_min, equipment_tier, equipment_tags[], trains_alone, excluded_exercise_ids[], preferred_exercise_ids[], affected_regions[], commitments[{day, commitment_type, intensity}], screening{version, outcome, answers{}, followups{}, guardian_consent, clearance_confirmed, date, conditions[]}, diet[], tight_budget, planned_absences[{absence_id, start_date, end_date, mode_id, note, status}]
column4: One document per user. Everything the generator reads at step 2 lives here.

column3: uid, version, split_id or day_structure, custom (bool), schedule{weekday: day_type}, created_at, active, superseded_by, generation_audit[{step, rule_ids_applied, choices}]
column4: Immutable once generated. Changes create a new version so history is never lost. The audit array records which rules fired, which is the debugging and liability trail.

column3: instance_id, program_id, uid, scheduled_date, day_type, status (planned|done|partial|missed|shifted), exercises[{slot_id, exercise_id, substituted_from, measurement_type, sets, reps_min, reps_max, duration_sec, work_sec, rest_sec, rounds, distance_m, load_kg_target, load_unit, assistance_kg, bodyweight_added_kg, band_level, tempo, side, rir_min}]
column4: One document per scheduled workout. This is what the app screen renders.

column3: log_id, instance_id, uid, exercise_id, set_number, measurement_type, load_kg, load_unit, reps_done, duration_sec_done, work_sec_done, rounds_done, distance_m_done, assistance_kg, band_level, tempo, side, rir_reported, timestamp, pain_flag, stop_symptom
column4: One document per completed set or round. measurement_type says which value fields are meaningful, so timed holds, intervals, carries and assisted lifts all log cleanly. Raw material for progression, F01-F03 and S06.

column3: current_load_kg, current_rep_target, last_progressed_at, sessions_at_current, stall_count, deload_count
column4: One document per user per exercise. Read at step 9, written after every completed instance.

column3: events[{from_id, to_id, reason (dislike | equipment | pain | skill), date}]
column4: Feeds exclusions: a dislike swap means the exercise is not served again, a pain swap also blocks it pending S06 clearance.

column3: stop_symptom_events[{symptom, date, resolved, cleared_by_professional}], deload_history[dates], missed_streaks{weekday: count}
